info.name: Integrity Shield API - Complete Test Suite
info.description:
Complete end-to-end testing collection for Integrity Shield with blockchain anchoring.

All endpoints use the /api/v1/ prefix.

SETUP:
1. Start the application: ./gradlew.bat bootRun
2. Set base_url variable (default: http://localhost:8080)
3. Run the sections in order: Files → Audit → Blockchain → E2E

item.name: 1. File Management
item.description: Upload files and manage file versions via /api/v1/files

formdata.description: Select any text file or document

request.description:
Upload first file to audit log. Returns 201 on success.

Automatically saves: contentHash, uploadTimestamp, fileIdentifier, storageVersionId, currentRoot, fileVersionId.

exec: pm.test('Merkle root changed after second upload', function() {

request.description: Upload second file. Merkle root should change (tree updated).

exec: pm.test('currentRoot unchanged (no new leaf added)', function() {

formdata.description: Upload same file as in step 1.1

request.description: Upload duplicate file with same content hash – should return 200 with status 'idempotent' without creating a new leaf. currentRoot should remain unchanged.

item.name: 2. Audit Verification
item.description: Query the audit log, Merkle proofs, and verify file inclusion via /api/v1/audit

request.description: Get current Merkle Root hash and leaf count. Response: { currentRoot, leafCount }

exec: pm.test('Has count and versions array', function() {
pm.test('Each version has required fields', function() {

request.description:
Get list of all uploaded file versions with their leaf indices.
Response: { count, versions: [{ id, fileIdentifier, storageVersionId, contentHash, leafIndex, uploadTimestamp }] }

exec: pm.test('Has fileIdentifier, count, and versions', function() {

request.description:
Get all versions for a specific file identifier.
Response: { fileIdentifier, count, versions: [...] }

exec: pm.test('Proof steps have siblingHash and position', function() {

request.description:
Get Merkle proof path for a file version. Uses {{file_version_id}} saved from step 1.1.
Response: { leafHash, leafIndex, proofPath: [{ siblingHash, position }], rootHash, generatedAt }
Get Merkle proof path for the second uploaded file version.

exec: pm.test('Verification result has required fields', function() {

request.description:
Verify that a file is included in the Merkle Tree.

All values come from step 1.1 automatically.
Request body fields: fileIdentifier, storageVersionId, contentHash, uploadTimestamp, knownRoot.
Response: { verified, fileIdentifier, storageVersionId, contentHash }

item.name: 3. Blockchain Anchoring
item.description: Anchor Merkle roots to blockchain and verify anchors via /api/v1/audit

request.description:
Get the most recently anchored checkpoint.
Returns: { checkpointId, rootHash, transactionHash, blockNumber, anchoredAt, networkName }

Get gas cost estimation for anchoring on testnet and mainnet.
Response: { testnet: { networkName, gasPriceWei, gasUsed, totalCostWei }, mainnetEstimate: { gasPriceWei, gasUsed, estimatedCostWei, estimatedCostEth, estimatedCostUsd, ethToUsdRate }, estimatedAt }

item.name: 4. End-to-End Test Scenario
item.description: Complete workflow test – run steps in order. Covers: upload → verify audit → anchor → verify anchor.

request.description: Check initial state. leafCount should be 0 or the number of previously uploaded files.
Upload first document.
Upload second document. Merkle root should differ from step 2.
Verify: both documents listed with correct leafIndex.

exec: pm.test('Has currentRoot and positive leafCount', function() {

request.description: Get the current Merkle root. Saves to collection variable for later steps.

item.name: E2E Step 6: Get Proof for Document A

request.description: Get proof for document A using the file version ID saved in step 2.
Verify that document A is included in the Merkle tree using all values saved from step 2.
Anchor the Merkle root to blockchain. Saves transactionHash for verification.
Verify the latest anchor matches the anchored values from step 8.

item.name: E2E Step 10: Verify Anchor on Blockchain

request.description: Verify the anchor using transactionHash from step 8. Should show isValid = true.
Get gas cost estimation for anchoring operations.
Simple health check – if this returns 200, the API is running.
Expected: 400 error – missing or empty file.
Expected: 404 – proof not found for non-existent file version.

item.name: Test Latest Anchor When None Exist (Should Return 404)

request.description: Expected: 404 – no anchored checkpoint exists yet (run on fresh database).

item.name: Test Verify Anchor with Invalid Data

request.description: Verify anchor with invalid data – should return isValid = false.

request.description: Verify inclusion with wrong root – should return verified = false.
